data.description: Bank of Japan says the balance of risks to the economic outlook is tilted toward weaker-than-expected outcomes.
Bank of Japan says inflation risks are leaning more toward upside surprises.
Bank of Japan says the proposal from Nakagawa, Takata and Tamura was rejected by the majority of the policy board.

Bank of Japan says Takata argued that the inflation objective has largely been met and that domestic price risks are already tilted to the upside, partly due to secondary effects from overseas price pressures.
Bank of Japan says Nakagawa noted that although uncertainty in the Middle East persists, recent economic conditions point to upward price risks under still-accommodative financial settings.
Bank of Japan says easy financial conditions are likely to continue supporting overall economic activity.
Bank of Japan says government support measures, including fuel subsidies, are expected to help cushion the economy.
Bank of Japan says monetary policy will be guided flexibly to ensure inflation is achieved in a stable and sustainable manner around the 2% target.
Bank of Japan says real interest rates are currently at very low levels by historical standards.
Bank of Japan says rising crude oil prices and weaker terms of trade are expected to reduce corporate earnings and erode households’ purchasing power.
The BOJ reached its policy decision with a 6–3 split vote.

data.title: Trip.com Wins the iF DESIGN AWARD 2026 for Outstanding Inclusive Design
data.description: In Singapore on April 27, 2026, it was announced that Trip.com's initiative, Trip for Everyone, has won the iF DESIGN AWARD 2026, a highly esteemed global design accolade. The iF DESIGN AWARD is widely acknowledged as one of the most prestigious design awards globally. Trip.com's project was recognized for its excellence in design, standing out among a competitive field. This recognition highlights Trip.com's commitment to creating inclusive and innovative travel experiences for all individuals.

data.title: Helical Fusion Launches Helix Program “Official Partners” to Build Japan’s Industrial Coalition for Commercial Fusion Energy
data.description: In Tokyo, Helical Fusion Co., Ltd. revealed the introduction of the Helix Program Official Partners, a strategic alliance aimed at uniting industrial collaborators to advance fusion energy from the lab to real-world deployment. The program is part of Helical Fusion's efforts to achieve commercially feasible fusion energy by the 2030s. This initiative centers on developing a Helical stellarator power plant.
The Philippine currency weakened to an all-time low of 60.90 per dollar.
